rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE annexes; avoid package-qualified names not present in the model.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference component ports or implementation details.
Do not introduce new data types, identifiers, or property references that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Use only named constants and visible features in AGREE contracts to maintain traceability and scope validity.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are visible in the component type scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not assume numeric or Boolean encodings for event ports without explicit data values in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
